game_name: Tom & Jerry: The Ultimate Game of Cat and Mouse!

design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate platforming stages as Jerry, collecting items and avoiding/defeating Tom with various weapons
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Evasion and combat against pursuing antagonist (Tom) across hazard-filled stages
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as Jerry the mouse, using household items as weapons to outwit and defeat Tom the cat across domestic environments
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Licensed IP platformer with asymmetric combat focus; emphasizes item-based problem solving rather than pure dexterity; regional difficulty balancing suggests developer commitment to accessibility for Japanese market

difficulty.lives_system: 3 lives per continue (Western) vs 5 lives per continue (Japanese)
difficulty.checkpointing: Health restoration after sub-levels in Japanese version only; Western version lacks this mercy mechanic
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: No difficulty selection documented; substantial regional variance suggests developer tuned for different skill expectations by market

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon/item selection system; players equip different tools (hammer, mothballs, meat cleaver, key, fuse) for specific stages and scenarios
progression.unlock_mechanism: Stage-based progression with specific items required for certain levels (e.g., key for Stage 5-2, fuse intended for Stage 1-1)
replayability_hooks: Multiple weapon options encourage different approaches to stages
Regional variants create incentive to experience Japanese vs Western versions
Unused items suggest content cut from final game, hinting at lost complexity

world_building_techniques: Domestic environments (kitchens, bathrooms) serve as stage backdrops
Tom & Jerry license provides implicit narrative context from animated series

boss_design.philosophy: Stage-culminating encounters against Tom; difficulty tuned regionally with Japanese version requiring fewer hits to defeat bosses

environmental_storytelling: Stage 4-1 features NES console in background (text erased in Japanese version)
Domestic kitchen/household settings reinforce Tom & Jerry IP authenticity

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Tom & Jerry NES article)
